tracks.meaning: to move or go across something, typically from one side to the other
a mark or line formed by two things intersecting at right angles
a Christian symbol consisting of two lines or bars intersecting at right angles
to oppose, thwart, or go against someone's wishes or plans
to pass or go from one place to another, especially in contact or conversation
a figure formed by two intersecting lines, typically at right angles
to breed animals or plants of different varieties or species together
to make someone annoyed or angry by irritating or challenging them
a hybrid organism resulting from breeding two different varieties or species
a punch delivered with a rotational motion of the fist and body in boxing
a journey or passage from one place to another, typically across terrain
to intersect or overlap in computer graphics or data visualization
to contradict or challenge testimony given by a witness in court
to move an actor or character from one side of the stage to the other
